data.title: Diamond Properties leases medical office space to GreenwichMD in Stamford, CT. Lease executed at 1351 Washington Boulevard. Dr. Susana Querido will lead the established medical and aesthetic practice.
data.description: In Stamford, Connecticut, Diamond Properties has secured a lease with MFQ Medical Services LLC, known as GreenwichMD, at 1351 Washington Boulevard. The lease was facilitated by Matt Mastrianni, Leasing Manager for Diamond Properties. GreenwichMD, led by board-certified physician Dr. Susana Querido, is a reputable medical and aesthetic practice offering a wide range of services focusing on longevity and anti-aging.

data.title: FDA clears eyonis® LCS Software as a Medical Device for lung cancer screening, appoints Oran Muduroglu as President of Median eyonis Inc. in the U.S.
data.description: In France, Median Technologies (FR0011049824, ALMDT) announced that Oran Muduroglu has been appointed as President of Median eyonis Inc., the Company's U.S. subsidiary. Median develops eyonis®, an AI-powered Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for early cancer diagnosis, along with providing AI-enhanced and central imaging services for oncology drug developers. Oran's role will involve leading the launch in the U.S. and overseeing operations.

data.title: WLFI token, backed by Trump family, jumps 23% before Mar-a-Lago crypto summit with 2 senators, CFTC chair, and top execs.
data.description: Despite the absence of US President Donald Trump, a notable event today will feature two senators, the chair of the CFTC, and key industry leaders.

data.title: Marimekko Corp bought back shares on 18/02/26.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the global economy is expected to grow by 4.5% in 2022, following a 3.5% growth in 2021. However, inflation rates are projected to increase to 4.2% this year, up from 2.5% last year. The report also highlights a rise in unemployment rates, with an estimated 180 million people expected to be out of work worldwide in 2022. Additionally, the report suggests that investments in renewable energy are crucial, with a target of $2 trillion needed to combat climate change effectively. Finally, the report mentions that the technology sector is predicted to expand by 6.9% in 2022, indicating promising growth opportunities in the industry.

data.title: Garmin (GRMN) reports Q4 2025 revenue of $2.12B, up 17% Y/Y, with Fitness segment revenue growing 42% to $765.84M.
data.description: Garmin Ltd's stock (NYSE: GRMN) saw a significant increase following the release of its fiscal fourth-quarter 2025 results. The company reported a quarterly revenue growth of 17% year-on-year, reaching $2.12 billion, surpassing the analyst consensus of $2.02 billion. The company's adjusted EPS of $2.79 also exceeded the analyst estimate of $2.40. The Fitness segment experienced a 42% increase in revenue to $765.84 million, driven by high demand for advanced wearables. Outdoor revenue remained steady at $627.61 million year-on-year. Aviation revenue rose by 16% year-on-year to $274.24 million, led by OEM and aftermarket categories. Marine revenue saw an 18% increase to $296.91 million, with growth in various categories, particularly chartplotters. Auto OEM revenue decreased by 3% year-on-year to $160.36 million.

data.title: Payment software company adds stablecoin settlement to its stack, combining USDG, USDP, USDC with ACH and wires. #Fintech #Innovation
data.description: The payment software firm has incorporated stablecoin settlement into its current payment system, merging USDG, USDP, and USDC with conventional payment methods. This update aims to enhance the efficiency and flexibility of transactions within the company, offering users more choices for settling payments.

data.title: JCDecaux introduces holistic tool globally to measure full campaign impact, enhancing visibility.
data.description: JCDecaux has introduced a comprehensive tool to evaluate the complete impact of campaigns globally. This tool will measure aspects that are not immediately visible, offering a more thorough analysis of campaign effectiveness. This initiative will be rolled out worldwide, emphasizing the importance of assessing all campaign elements for an accurate evaluation.

data.title: Palo Alto Networks stock slides as Q2 results disappoint analysts. Various price targets reduced, with organic NGS ARR up 28% YoY.
data.description: Shares of Palo Alto Networks Inc (NASDAQ: PANW) plummeted after the company's fiscal second-quarter results. Various analysts adjusted their ratings and price targets for the company. Needham analyst Mike Cikos maintained a Buy rating but decreased the price target from $230 to $200. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs analyst Gabriela Borges reiterated a Buy rating and cut the price target from $240 to $224. Scotiabank analyst Patrick Colville reaffirmed a Sector Outperform rating, adjusting the price target from $228 to $180. DA Davidson analyst Rudy Kessinger reiterated a Buy rating, with the price target lowered from $210 to $190. Rosenblatt Securities' Catharine Trebnick and BTIG's Gray Powell maintained Buy ratings with price targets of $225 and $200, respectively. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives kept an Outperform rating and price target of $225, while Citizens JMP Securities analyst Trevor Walsh reiterated a Market Outperform rating and a $250 price target. Needham highlighted Palo Alto Networks' solid results, with organic NGS ARR growing 28% YoY to $6.14 billion (excluding $200 million from Chronosphere). The company's guidance for NGS ARR in the fiscal third quarter was at $7.94-$7.96 billion, including $1.47 billion from acquisitions, aligning with consensus on an organic basis. On the other hand, Goldman Sachs noted that Palo Alto Networks reported NGS ARR 3% above estimates, largely due to the Chronosphere acquisition. The stock faced pressure due to limited organic growth and "inorganic moving pieces."
